The Divergent Series: Insurgent (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $130,179,072Details
International Box Office $164,896,810
Worldwide Box Office $295,075,882
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $20,740,836
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $14,798,894
Total $35,539,730
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The stakes are raised for Tris as she searches for allies and answers in the ruins of a futuristic Chicago. Tris and Four are now fugitives on the run, hunted by Jeanine, the leader of the power-hungry Erudite elite. Racing against time, they must find out what Tris’s family sacrificed their lives to protect, and why the Erudite leaders will do anything to stop them. Haunted by her past choices but desperate to protect the ones she loves, Tris, with Four at her side, faces one impossible challenge after another as they unlock the truth about the past and ultimately the future of their world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$52,263,680 (40.1% of total gross)
Legs:2.49 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:44.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$110,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,875 opening theaters/3,875 max. theaters, 5.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $184,690,590

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 20th, 2015 (Wide) by Lionsgate, released as The Divergent Serires: Insurgent
March 20th, 2015 (IMAX) by Lionsgate, released as The Divergent Serires: Insurgent
International Releases: March 18th, 2015 (Wide), released as Divergente 2: L'insurrection (France)
March 18th, 2015 (Wide), released as Insurgent (Belgium)
March 19th, 2015 (Wide), released as Insurgent (United Arab Emirates)
March 19th, 2015 (Wide), released as Insurgent (Uruguay)
March 19th, 2015 (Wide), released as Insurgent (Slovenia)
Video Release: July 21st, 2015 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment, released as Insurgent
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense violence and action throughout, some sensuality, thematic elements and brief language.
(Rating bulletin 2357, 1/21/2015)
Running Time: 118 minutes
Franchise: The Divergent Series
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Coming of Age, Cover Up, Faked Death, Fugitive / On the Run, Near Death Experience, Non-Chronological, Political, Suicide, Tortured for Information
Social setting: Dystopia
Source material: Young Adult Book Adaptation
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Summit Entertainment, Red Wagon Entertainment, Mandeville Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

The stakes are raised for Tris as she searches for allies and answers in the ruins of a futuristic Chicago. Tris and Four are now fugitives on the run, hunted by Jeanine, the leader of the power-hungry Erudite elite. Racing against time, they must find out what Tris’s family sacrificed their lives to protect, and why the Erudite leaders will do anything to stop them. Haunted by her past choices but desperate to protect the ones she loves, Tris, with Four at her side, faces one impossible challenge after another as they unlock the truth about the past and ultimately the future of their world.
